title: Yope Secures $12.3 Million: A Social Network Without Algorithms, Ads, or Publicity
excerpt: As the face of social media quietly shifts from "staying in touch with friends" to a "big entertainment square," a startup called Yope is quietly doing somethin
content_html:
As the face of social media quietly shifts from "staying in touch with friends" to a "big entertainment square," a startup called Yope is quietly doing something different — it's betting on a different future: a social network without algorithms, ads, or public content. This $12.3 million funding round led by Northzone has given this idea an extra boost.

Yope's approach is to bypass giants like Facebook, TikTok, Snapchat, and Instagram, and build what it calls a "micro-community" — small, private circles where friends and family can interact, share photos, videos, messages, and soon, play games together. Without algorithmic feeds or public squares, this concept isn't new, but few apps have truly grown without relying on creator content to keep users engaged. Yope's bet is to reconnect people who are already friends in the real world, making it both a communication tool and a place to share life.

On Yope, accounts are default private, there is no algorithmic feed, and it doesn't rely on ads but focuses on offering a premium experience for heavy users through a subscription model. Bahram Ismailau, co-founder and CEO based in London, said that the team conducted over 100,000 interviews with the help of AI tools to understand how young people around the world use social networks, which led them to see the potential of a new kind of platform. They found that about 30% of young people have so-called "photo dump" or "spam" side accounts, sharing more casual photos with a small group of real-life friends; more people simply don't post anything publicly, maintaining relationships only through private messages. "If you don't want to be a celebrity, young people have nowhere to express themselves," Ismailau told TechCrunch, but they still want to express themselves, just with more privacy. Thus came Yope — an AI-native social platform for young people and the next generation.

He specifically draws a line: the team doesn't believe in using AI to generate content, but only uses AI as a tool to help people build better connections. This includes AI-generated mini-games that users can play with friends (coming online in about a month), and also using AI to match offline meetups, such as buying tickets to shows or finding restaurants that show sports. Users build their own homepages by sharing photos, which can be turned into stickers; they aren't organized by albums, but are spread out almost randomly on everyone's "wall," similar to a collage. Soon, users will also be able to add interests, favorite music, and mini-games, and personalize their style with custom colors and wallpapers — a vibe reminiscent of Myspace, which allowed users to customize their personal pages freely.

Yope also leverages the power of established platforms: in-app private messaging, highlights recaps (generated by AI), and widgets that move friends' photos to the lock screen are now standard features. The onboarding process guides users step by step to activate functions, authorize image reading, find friends, and encourage them to start private chats. This combination seems to have worked: Yope now has nearly 15 million registered users, sharing between 10 million to 20 million pieces of content weekly (each photo, video, or sticker counts), with more than half of users opening the app at least five days a week, making them heavy users; about 20% of active users have also brought in older family members.

sentences.en: They found that about 30% of young people have so-called "photo dump" or "spam" side accounts, sharing more casual photos with a small group of real-life friends; more people simply don't post anything publicly, maintaining relationships only through private messages.
sentences.zh: 他们发现，大约 30% 的年轻人拥有所谓的“照片转储”或“垃圾邮件”账户，与一小群现实生活中的朋友分享更多休闲照片；更多的人根本不公开发布任何内容，仅通过私人消息来维持关系。

sentences.en: "If you don't want to be a celebrity, young people have nowhere to express themselves," Ismailau told TechCrunch, but they still want to express themselves, just with more privacy.
sentences.zh: 伊斯梅劳告诉 TechCrunch：“如果你不想成为名人，年轻人就没有地方表达自己。”但他们仍然想表达自己，只是需要更多的隐私。

sentences.en: Thus came Yope — an AI-native social platform for young people and the next generation.
sentences.zh: 于是Yope诞生了——一个面向年轻人和下一代的人工智能原生社交平台。

sentences.en: He specifically draws a line: the team doesn't believe in using AI to generate content, but only uses AI as a tool to help people build better connections.
sentences.zh: 他特别划了一条线：团队并不相信用AI来生成内容，而只是将AI作为一种工具来帮助人们建立更好的联系。

sentences.en: This includes AI-generated mini-games that users can play with friends (coming online in about a month), and also using AI to match offline meetups, such as buying tickets to shows or finding restaurants that show sports.
sentences.zh: 这包括人工智能生成的迷你游戏，用户可以与朋友一起玩（大约一个月后上线），还使用人工智能来匹配线下聚会，例如购买演出门票或寻找播放体育节目的餐厅。

sentences.en: Users build their own homepages by sharing photos, which can be turned into stickers; they aren't organized by albums, but are spread out almost randomly on everyone's "wall," similar to a collage.
sentences.zh: 用户通过分享照片建立自己的主页，这些照片可以变成贴纸；它们不是按专辑组织的，而是几乎随机地分布在每个人的“墙上”，类似于拼贴画。

sentences.en: Soon, users will also be able to add interests, favorite music, and mini-games, and personalize their style with custom colors and wallpapers — a vibe reminiscent of Myspace, which allowed users to customize their personal pages freely.
sentences.zh: 很快，用户还可以添加兴趣、喜欢的音乐和迷你游戏，并通过自定义颜色和壁纸来个性化自己的风格——这让人想起 Myspace，它允许用户自由定制自己的个人页面。

blocks.html_en: Yope also leverages the power of established platforms: in-app private messaging, highlights recaps (generated by AI), and widgets that move friends' photos to the lock screen are now standard features. The onboarding process guides users step by step to activate functions, authorize image reading, find friends, and encourage them to start private chats. This combination seems to have worked: Yope now has nearly 15 million registered users, sharing between 10 million to 20 million pieces of content weekly (each photo, video, or sticker counts), with more than half of users opening the app at least five days a week, making them heavy users; about 20% of active users have also brought in older family members.

sentences.en: Yope also leverages the power of established platforms: in-app private messaging, highlights recaps (generated by AI), and widgets that move friends' photos to the lock screen are now standard features.
sentences.zh: Yope 还利用了现有平台的力量：应用内私人消息传递、亮点回顾（由人工智能生成）以及将朋友的照片移动到锁定屏幕的小部件现已成为标准功能。

sentences.en: The onboarding process guides users step by step to activate functions, authorize image reading, find friends, and encourage them to start private chats.
sentences.zh: 引导流程一步步引导用户激活功能、授权看图、寻找好友、鼓励私聊。

sentences.en: This combination seems to have worked: Yope now has nearly 15 million registered users, sharing between 10 million to 20 million pieces of content weekly (each photo, video, or sticker counts), with more than half of users opening the app at least five days a week, making them heavy users; about 20% of active users have also brought in older family members.
sentences.zh: 这种组合似乎很有效：Yope 现在拥有近 1500 万注册用户，每周分享 1000 万到 2000 万条内容（每张照片、视频或贴纸都算在内），超过一半的用户每周至少打开该应用五天，成为重度用户；大约 20% 的活跃用户还引入了年长的家庭成员。

content_html_zh:
随着社交媒体的面貌悄然从“与朋友保持联系”转变为“大型娱乐广场”，一家名为 Yope 的初创公司正在悄悄做一些不同的事情——它押注于一个不同的未来：一个没有算法、广告或公共内容的社交网络。 由 Northzone 牵头的这轮 1230 万美元融资进一步推动了这一想法。

Yope 的方法是绕过 Facebook、TikTok、Snapchat 和 Instagram 等巨头，建立所谓的“微社区”——朋友和家人可以在其中互动、分享照片、视频、消息，以及很快一起玩游戏的小型私人圈子。 如果没有算法提要或公共广场，这个概念并不新鲜，但很少有应用程序能够在不依赖创作者内容来保持用户参与的情况下真正发展起来。 Yope 的目标是让现实世界中已经是朋友的人们重新建立联系，使其既成为沟通工具，又成为分享生活的场所。

在 Yope 上，帐户默认是私有的，没有算法提要，它不依赖广告，而是专注于通过订阅模式为重度用户提供优质体验。 驻伦敦的联合创始人兼首席执行官巴赫拉姆·伊斯梅劳 (Bahram Ismailau) 表示，团队借助人工智能工具进行了超过 10 万次采访，以了解世界各地的年轻人如何使用社交网络，这让他们看到了一种新型平台的潜力。 他们发现，大约 30% 的年轻人拥有所谓的“照片转储”或“垃圾邮件”账户，与一小群现实生活中的朋友分享更多休闲照片；更多的人根本不公开发布任何内容，仅通过私人消息来维持关系。 伊斯梅劳告诉 TechCrunch：“如果你不想成为名人，年轻人就没有地方表达自己。”但他们仍然想表达自己，只是需要更多的隐私。 于是Yope诞生了——一个面向年轻人和下一代的人工智能原生社交平台。

他特别划了一条线：团队并不相信用AI来生成内容，而只是将AI作为一种工具来帮助人们建立更好的联系。 这包括人工智能生成的迷你游戏，用户可以与朋友一起玩（大约一个月后上线），还使用人工智能来匹配线下聚会，例如购买演出门票或寻找播放体育节目的餐厅。 用户通过分享照片建立自己的主页，这些照片可以变成贴纸；它们不是按专辑组织的，而是几乎随机地分布在每个人的“墙上”，类似于拼贴画。 很快，用户还可以添加兴趣、喜欢的音乐和迷你游戏，并通过自定义颜色和壁纸来个性化自己的风格——这让人想起 Myspace，它允许用户自由定制自己的个人页面。

Yope 还利用了现有平台的力量：应用内私人消息传递、亮点回顾（由人工智能生成）以及将朋友的照片移动到锁定屏幕的小部件现已成为标准功能。 引导流程一步步引导用户激活功能、授权看图、寻找好友、鼓励私聊。 这种组合似乎很有效：Yope 现在拥有近 1500 万注册用户，每周分享 1000 万到 2000 万条内容（每张照片、视频或贴纸都算在内），超过一半的用户每周至少打开该应用五天，成为重度用户；大约 20% 的活跃用户还引入了年长的家庭成员。
